chapter: Class 10 Economics - Chapter 4: Globalisation and the Indian Economy

questions.question: What is globalisation?
questions.answer: Globalisation is the integration of countries through trade, investment and technology.

questions.question: What are Multinational Corporations (MNCs)?
questions.answer: MNCs are companies that operate in more than one country.

questions.question: What is Foreign Direct Investment (FDI)?
questions.answer: FDI is investment made by an MNC in production or business in another country.

questions.question: Why do MNCs set up production in different countries?
questions.answer: To reduce costs and increase profits.

questions.question: What is foreign trade?
questions.answer: Foreign trade is the exchange of goods and services between countries.

questions.question: What are exports?
questions.answer: Exports are goods and services sold to other countries.

questions.question: What are imports?
questions.answer: Imports are goods and services bought from other countries.

questions.question: What are trade barriers?
questions.answer: Trade barriers are restrictions such as taxes imposed on imports.

questions.question: What is a tariff?
questions.answer: A tariff is a tax imposed on imported goods.

questions.question: What is liberalisation?
questions.answer: Liberalisation is the removal of trade barriers and government restrictions.

questions.question: In which year did India adopt economic reforms?
questions.answer: India adopted economic reforms in 1991.

questions.question: What is the WTO?
questions.answer: The WTO is an international organisation that sets rules for global trade.

questions.question: When was the WTO established?
questions.answer: The WTO was established in 1995.

questions.question: How does technology support globalisation?
questions.answer: Technology improves communication and transport, connecting markets globally.

questions.question: How has globalisation benefited consumers?
questions.answer: It has provided more choices and better quality goods.

questions.question: How are small producers affected by globalisation?
questions.answer: They face strong competition from large MNCs.

questions.question: What is meant by investment abroad?
questions.answer: Investment abroad means investing money in businesses located in other countries.

questions.question: Why did India remove trade barriers in 1991?
questions.answer: To encourage foreign investment and integrate with the global economy.

questions.question: What is meant by global production?
questions.answer: Global production is when different stages of production are spread across countries.

questions.question: Name one sector in India that benefited from globalisation.
questions.answer: The IT and service sector benefited significantly from globalisation.

questions.question: What is meant by competition in the market?
questions.answer: Competition means many producers sell similar goods, giving consumers choices.

questions.question: How does foreign trade connect markets?
questions.answer: Foreign trade allows goods produced in one country to be sold in another.

questions.question: Why do MNCs prefer countries with cheap labour?
questions.answer: Because it reduces production costs and increases profits.

questions.question: What is meant by global market?
questions.answer: A global market is a market where goods and services are sold internationally.

questions.question: What is one challenge of globalisation?
questions.answer: It may lead to job insecurity for small producers and workers.
